redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: Focused OAuth tests failed because access_token and refresh_token were stored as plaintext in tiktok_credentials.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: Focused OAuth encryption tests passed after repo-level encryption was added.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Broader OAuth, callback, verify-connection, deploy-config, and backend deploy tests passed; IDE lints clean.

commands.outputSummary: 2 failed: persisted database token columns matched plaintext token fixtures.
2 passed after encrypting token writes and decrypting repo reads.

implementationSummary: Added issue #259 context for the TikTok OAuth token flow, then implemented encrypted at-rest storage for TikTok access/refresh tokens in TikTokCredentialRepo while preserving plaintext values for existing repo callers and token refresh flows. Updated focused tests, test encryption fixtures, deploy env/runbooks, and TikTok auth docs for the preferred token encryption key.
assumptions: The existing shops + tiktok_credentials schema remains the current persistence boundary; the issue comment's merchant_connections wording maps to this repo's existing credential model until a separate schema rename is explicitly scoped.
TIKTOK_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY is the preferred production secret; TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY or TIKTOK_APP_SECRET fallback keeps App Review/local OAuth persistence operational with existing env setup.
Existing plaintext rows can still be read because decrypt_token accepts unprefixed legacy values.
risks: Rotating the token encryption key without re-encrypting existing enc:v1 values will make encrypted tokens unreadable.
The implementation relies on cryptography supplied by the existing pyjwt[crypto] dependency chain; requirements do not list cryptography directly.
Existing validation artifact predates this continuation and should be regenerated if this branch is going through the full validate phase.
